title: Parahebe brassii
intro: Parahebe brassii is a flowering plant species native to New Zealand, belonging to the mint family Lamiaceae and commonly known as the 'New Zealand parahebe'. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and protecting against cell damage. Parahebe brassii contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The plant is traditionally used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often consumed to support immune function and overall well-being.
taxonomy: Parahebe brassii is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae, but now it is classified under the family Plantaginaceae. This classification is based on the recent reorganization of plant taxonomy. The genus Parahebe is native to New Zealand and Australia. It is a small, herbaceous plant that produces blue or white flowers. The classification of Parahebe brassii reflects the ongoing efforts to refine and update plant classifications based on molecular and morphological research.
